Title: Research progress in the biosynthesis and metabolism of methoxypyrazines in grape and wine

Pathway_Genes.Pathway_Branch_or_Subpathway: Final O-methylation step of hydroxypyrazines to methoxypyrazines
Pathway_Genes.Terminal_Metabolite_Function: Imparts herbaceous/green/vegetal aroma to grapes and wine
Pathway_Genes.Catalyzed_Reaction_Description: Transfers a methyl group from SAM to hydroxypyrazines, converting IBHP to IBMP and IPHP to IPMP
Pathway_Genes.Rate_Limiting_or_Key_Control_Step: Confirmed terminal determinant; enzymatic methylation of HPs to MPs is described as an important factor determining MPs level in grape berries, but strict rate-limiting status not established
Pathway_Genes.Expression_Pattern_of_Source_Species: Expressed in developing berry skin and flesh; timing of expression corresponds to period of MP accumulation and maximum IBMP accumulation pre-veraison
Pathway_Genes.Expression_Pattern_of_Applied_Species: Expressed pre-veraison in grape berries; decreases later during ripening
Pathway_Genes.Terminal_Metabolite_Accumulation_Site: IBMP accumulates mainly in grape stems and berry skins
Pathway_Genes.Core_Phenotypic_Effect: Promotes formation and accumulation of IBMP, with stronger activity toward IBHP than IPHP
Pathway_Genes.Core_Validation_Method: Gene cloning; enzyme activity assay with HP substrates and SAM; expression analysis during berry development; metabolite profiling; comparative catalytic analysis
Pathway_Genes.Summary_Key_Findings_of_Core_Gene: 1. VvOMT1 encodes a class II O-methyltransferase that methylates IBHP to IBMP and IPHP to IPMP. 2. Its expression in berry skin and flesh coincides with the period of methoxypyrazine accumulation before veraison. 3. VvOMT1 shows stronger activity toward IBHP and was found to be more catalytically efficient than VvOMT2, supporting a major role in grape MP biosynthesis.
Pathway_Genes.Breeding_or_Engineering_Application_Valu: Useful target to reduce herbaceous aroma in cultivars with excessive IBMP by lowering expression/activity, or to modulate cultivar-specific MP profiles; can be combined with allele selection or editing
Pathway_Genes.Omics_Data: QTL mapping mentioned in the review for related OMT candidates, but no accession provided for VvOMT1
Pathway_Genes.Other_Important_Info: Also prefers quercetin over HP substrates, with 100-500 fold higher activity toward quercetin than HPs; therefore may have dual substrate preference
Pathway_Genes.Potential_Tradeoffs: Because VvOMT1 also methylates quercetin efficiently, engineering may affect flavonol metabolism

Pathway_Genes.Pathway_Branch_or_Subpathway: Final O-methylation step of hydroxypyrazines to methoxypyrazines
Pathway_Genes.Terminal_Metabolite_Function: Imparts herbaceous/green/vegetal aroma to grapes and wine
Pathway_Genes.Catalyzed_Reaction_Description: Transfers a methyl group from SAM to hydroxypyrazines, converting IPHP to IPMP and IBHP to IBMP
Pathway_Genes.Rate_Limiting_or_Key_Control_Step: Confirmed terminal-step enzyme; contributes to MP levels but appears less catalytically efficient than VvOMT1
Pathway_Genes.Expression_Pattern_of_Source_Species: Highest expression in roots; also expressed pre-veraison in grape berries
Pathway_Genes.Expression_Pattern_of_Applied_Species: Highest in roots, possibly explaining high root IPMP content
Pathway_Genes.Terminal_Metabolite_Accumulation_Site: IPMP enriched in roots; IBMP mainly in stems and berry skins
Pathway_Genes.Core_Phenotypic_Effect: Promotes IPMP formation preferentially and contributes to methoxypyrazine accumulation
Pathway_Genes.Core_Validation_Method: Gene cloning; enzyme activity assay with HP substrates and SAM; expression profiling; comparative catalytic analysis; structure-function analysis
Pathway_Genes.Summary_Key_Findings_of_Core_Gene: 1. VvOMT2 encodes a class II O-methyltransferase that methylates HP precursors to MPs. 2. It shows higher activity toward IPHP than toward IBHP and is highly expressed in roots, consistent with root IPMP accumulation. 3. Its catalytic efficiency is lower than VvOMT1, likely due to steric effects involving residues H272 and M182.
Pathway_Genes.Breeding_or_Engineering_Application_Valu: Potential target to alter root/berry MP composition and reduce undesirable vegetal aroma; active-site residues H272 and M182 were proposed as engineering targets
Pathway_Genes.Other_Important_Info: Like VvOMT1, prefers quercetin over HP substrates; residues H272 and M182 were proposed to underlie lower catalytic efficiency
Pathway_Genes.Potential_Tradeoffs: Editing may influence flavonol methylation due to substrate preference for quercetin

Pathway_Genes.Pathway_Branch_or_Subpathway: Final O-methylation step candidate for HP to MP conversion
Pathway_Genes.Terminal_Metabolite_Function: Imparts herbaceous/green/vegetal aroma to grapes and wine
Pathway_Genes.Catalyzed_Reaction_Description: Likely methylates hydroxypyrazine precursors to form methoxypyrazines
Pathway_Genes.Rate_Limiting_or_Key_Control_Step: Proposed major role in berry MP biosynthesis; not fully functionally established in this review
Pathway_Genes.Expression_Pattern_of_Source_Species: Expressed in Cabernet Sauvignon fruit during IBMP accumulation; not expressed in fruit of Pinot varieties lacking IBMP
Pathway_Genes.Expression_Pattern_of_Applied_Species: Berry/fruit expression associated with IBMP-producing cultivars
Pathway_Genes.Core_Phenotypic_Effect: Likely promotes berry IBMP biosynthesis and may explain varietal differences in MP aroma contribution
Pathway_Genes.Core_Validation_Method: QTL mapping; expression analysis across cultivars
Pathway_Genes.Summary_Key_Findings_of_Core_Gene: 1. VvOMT3 was identified as a candidate methyltransferase gene through QTL mapping. 2. It is expressed in Cabernet Sauvignon fruit during IBMP accumulation but absent from Pinot fruit lacking IBMP. 3. It was suggested to have high affinity for HP precursors and to play a major role in berry MP biosynthesis.
Pathway_Genes.Breeding_or_Engineering_Application_Valu: Promising marker or editing target for cultivar-specific reduction of IBMP-related green aroma
Pathway_Genes.Other_Important_Info: Candidate role inferred from genetic mapping and expression, not described as fully biochemically validated here

Pathway_Genes.Pathway_Branch_or_Subpathway: Final O-methylation step candidate for HP to MP conversion
Pathway_Genes.Terminal_Metabolite_Function: Imparts herbaceous/green/vegetal aroma to grapes and wine
Pathway_Genes.Catalyzed_Reaction_Description: Encodes a product capable of methylating hydroxypyrazines to form methoxypyrazines
Pathway_Genes.Core_Phenotypic_Effect: Contributes to HP methylation capacity in grape
Pathway_Genes.Core_Validation_Method: Gene identification; functional statement in review that all four OMT genes encode products capable of methylating HPs
Pathway_Genes.Summary_Key_Findings_of_Core_Gene: 1. VvOMT4 is one of four grape OMT genes isolated in relation to MP biosynthesis. 2. Its encoded product is reported to methylate HPs to form MPs. 3. Specific developmental and tissue roles remain unclear in this review.
Pathway_Genes.Breeding_or_Engineering_Application_Valu: Secondary candidate for fine-tuning MP content once its specific contribution is resolved
Pathway_Genes.Omics_Data: QTL mapping mentioned for candidate identification; no accession reported
Pathway_Genes.Other_Important_Info: Evidence is less detailed than for VvOMT1 and VvOMT2
